safety_tips.tip: Tipping is a norm. Restaurant waiters expect 18%-25% of total bill as tips. For taxi rides, 10%-15% tips are customary.
Electric outlets run at 110V. Most phone/laptop chargers work between 110-220V but check your charger. Buy pin converters so Indian chargers work in the US.

emergency_contacts.notes: One number for any emergencies including medical emergencies and ambulance. Accessible from anywhere, even where there doesn't appear to be any cell signal

connectivity_tips.tip: You can buy a sim card from TSIM. They offer both physical and eSIMs. If getting two sims, recommend getting a T-mobile one and an AT&T one.

transport_options.description: For other places outside New York, use taxis as public transport isn't great elsewhere

health_tips.tip: Travel Insurance recommended with $100,000 coverage for each family member. Tata AIG has a plan for Rs. 8370 with $100 deductible. Alternative: visitorscoverage.com Patriot America Plus plan with zero deductible at $200.
Most places are pretty dry and it's easy to get sunburnt, so carry and apply sunscreen (SPF 30) when out in the Sun. Apply sunscreen in Florida as well even though it's more humid.
Carry doctors prescriptions/travel prescriptions for medicines (except over the counter meds)
